name: Southwest Meat Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup green bell peppers, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry bacon until crisp; break into large pieces.
2. Chill 1/3 cup bacon drippings until firm to use for crust, or use vegetable shortening instead.
3. Brown ground beef in a large skillet and drain well. Stir in corn, green bell peppers, onion, cornmeal, oregano, chili powder, salt, pepper, tomato sauce, and half the green chiles. Keep warm while preparing pie crust.
4. CRUST: Combine 1 cup flour and 2 tablespoons cornmeal. Cut in bacon drippings with two knives or a pastry blender until the mixture is the size of small peas. Sprinkle 3-4 tablespoons water over the mixture; stir with a fork until the dough holds together. Form into a ball, flatten to 1/2 inch thick, and keep edges smooth.
5. Roll out on a floured surface to a diameter 1-1/2 inch larger than an inverted 9-inch pie plate. Fit into the pie plate and fold edges to form a rim; flute edges.
6. Place the ground beef mixture into the pie crust. Bake in a preheated 425-degree oven for 25 minutes.
7. Combine egg, milk, salt, dry mustard, Worcestershire sauce, half the sliced olives, 2 tablespoons of green chiles, and shredded cheese. Spread on top of the beef mixture. Top with bacon and remaining olives. Bake for 5 minutes or until the cheese is melted and bubbly. Let stand 10 minutes before slicing.
